task_completion.description: Create a new dashboard named 'ORM Efficiency Analysis'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'ORM Efficiency Analysis' at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Raw Traffic Rate' with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Raw Traffic Rate'. In its configuration or legend, verify the target is exactly 'nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count)'.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Database CPU' with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Database CPU'. In its configuration or legend, verify the target is exactly 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization'.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Efficiency Index' with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Efficiency Index'. Verify the target uses the 'divideSeries' function combining the raw traffic rate and database CPU metrics: 'divideSeries(nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count), servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization)'.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Efficiency Degradation' with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the title 'Efficiency Degradation'. Verify the target includes both the current efficiency index and a 'timeShift' function comparing it to the previous day ('1d').
Look for a 'Dashboard saved' confirmation notification, or verify the dashboard appears in the saved dashboards list/menu in the Graphite UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Graphite web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the actual Graphite web UI being used to construct the dashboard, not a terminal window or text editor modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Graphs display actual rendered data lines
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs in the dashboard should show plotted lines or data points, indicating that the metrics were successfully queried from the backend, rather than displaying errors or empty grids.

integrity.description: Agent did not just type the answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not simply open a text editor (like Notepad, gedit, or nano) and paste the required graph titles and targets into it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'ORM Efficiency Analysis'. The dashboard must contain exactly four graphs with the following titles: 'Raw Traffic Rate', 'Database CPU', 'Efficiency Index', and 'Efficiency Degradation'.
